vocabulary.name: World Air Quality Index (WAQI) API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain vocabulary for the WAQI JSON API covering air quality terminology, pollutants, indices, and API concepts.

terms.label: Air Quality Index
terms.definition: A standardized index used by government agencies to communicate how polluted the air currently is or how polluted it is forecast to become. Scale: 0-50 Good, 51-100 Moderate, 101-150 Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups, 151-200 Unhealthy, 201-300 Very Unhealthy, 301+ Hazardous.

terms.label: Station Index
terms.definition: The unique numeric identifier assigned to a monitoring station within the WAQI network. Used to reference specific stations in API calls (e.g., @8397).

terms.label: Individual Air Quality Index
terms.definition: Per-pollutant AQI sub-index values. Each pollutant (PM2.5, PM10, NO2, CO, SO2, O3) has its own sub-index calculated independently. The overall AQI is the maximum of all sub-indices.

terms.label: Dominant Pollutant
terms.definition: The pollutant with the highest individual AQI sub-index value, which drives the overall AQI calculation.

terms.label: PM2.5
terms.definition: Particulate Matter with an aerodynamic diameter of 2.5 micrometers or less. Considered the most dangerous common air pollutant due to its ability to penetrate deep into the lungs and bloodstream.

terms.label: PM10
terms.definition: Particulate Matter with an aerodynamic diameter of 10 micrometers or less. Includes dust, pollen, mold, and combustion particles.

terms.label: Nitrogen Dioxide
terms.definition: Nitrogen Dioxide (NO2), a reddish-brown gas primarily from vehicle emissions and power plants. Contributes to smog formation and respiratory problems.

terms.label: Carbon Monoxide
terms.definition: Carbon Monoxide (CO), a colorless and odorless gas produced by incomplete combustion of carbon-containing fuels. Reduces blood oxygen-carrying capacity.

terms.label: Sulfur Dioxide
terms.definition: Sulfur Dioxide (SO2), produced primarily from burning fossil fuels containing sulfur. Can cause respiratory irritation and contributes to acid rain.

terms.label: Ozone
terms.definition: Ground-level Ozone (O3), formed by sunlight reacting with NOx and VOCs. At ground level it is a harmful pollutant causing respiratory issues, unlike protective stratospheric ozone.

terms.label: Temperature
terms.definition: Ambient air temperature recorded at the monitoring station.

terms.label: Humidity
terms.definition: Relative humidity percentage recorded at the monitoring station.

terms.label: Pressure
terms.definition: Atmospheric pressure recorded at the monitoring station.

terms.label: Wind Speed
terms.definition: Wind speed recorded at the monitoring station.

terms.label: Wind Gust
terms.definition: Peak wind gust speed recorded at the monitoring station.

terms.label: UV Index
terms.definition: Ultraviolet Index — a measure of the strength of ultraviolet radiation from the sun at a particular place on a given day. Scale: 1-2 Low, 3-5 Moderate, 6-7 High, 8-10 Very High, 11+ Extreme.

terms.label: API Token
terms.definition: Authentication credential required for all WAQI API requests. Obtained for free from the Air Quality Open Data Platform at https://aqicn.org/data-platform/token/ for non-commercial use.

terms.label: Geographic Coordinates
terms.definition: Latitude and longitude pair representing the geographic position of a monitoring station or query point. Provided as [latitude, longitude] array.

terms.label: Bounding Box
terms.definition: Rectangular geographic area defined by four coordinates: lat_north,lng_west,lat_south,lng_east. Used in map bounds queries to retrieve all stations within the area.

terms.label: Data Attribution
terms.definition: Reference to the environmental monitoring agency or organization that collected and provided the raw air quality data used to calculate the AQI.

terms.label: Air Quality Forecast
terms.definition: Predicted air quality values for upcoming days (typically 3-8 days), providing daily minimum, maximum, and average AQI values per pollutant.

terms.label: City Feed
terms.definition: The primary API endpoint returning real-time air quality data for a specific city or named monitoring station.

terms.label: Geolocalized Feed
terms.definition: API endpoint that returns air quality data for the nearest monitoring station to a provided latitude/longitude coordinate pair.

terms.label: IP-Based Feed
terms.definition: API endpoint that uses the requesting client's IP address for geolocation to return air quality data for the nearest monitoring station.

terms.label: Map Bounds Query
terms.definition: API endpoint returning all monitoring stations within a specified geographic bounding box, suitable for rendering on interactive maps.
